claims.claim: Aliyah passport recommended valid at least 2 years
claims.raw_snippet: valid for at least another 2 years

claims.claim: Effective Sept 30 2024, marriage certificate must show pre-marriage civil status of both spouses
claims.raw_snippet: From Sept 30, 2024 onward, must show pre-marriage status

claims.claim: From Nov 2024 Shinui Ma'amad applicants (aliyah from within Israel) need background checks from every country lived in for 6+ months
claims.raw_snippet: From Nov 2024 onward, people who undergo Shinui Ma'amad - Aliyah from within Israel...will be asked to present background checks from all countries where they have lived for more than one year

claims.claim: Background check required from country of birth, every country of continuous residence over 1 year after age 14, and every country of current citizenship
claims.raw_snippet: Your country of birth; Any country where you've lived continuously for over 1 year (after age 14); Any country of which you currently hold citizenship

claims.claim: Free Ulpan Aleph entitlement is 500 hours within 18 months of aliyah (widely-quoted Misrad HaAliyah policy figure; verify current entitlement with the absorbing Misrad HaKlita branch since hour caps have been adjusted periodically)
claims.raw_snippet: Ulpan Aleph, free Hebrew course for new olim, around 500 hours of subsidized study within the first 18 months

claims.claim: Ulpan requires 80% attendance minimum to stay in the subsidized track
claims.raw_snippet: requires 80% attendance

claims.claim: Second subsidized Ulpan course available within 10 years of aliyah; only one of the two can be at a private/recognized institution
claims.raw_snippet: Second course available within 10 years (only one can be private)

claims.claim: Ezrach Oleh applicants need proof of residence outside Israel for the past 7 years and a 7-year table of entries and exits
claims.raw_snippet: Proof of residence outside Israel for the past 7 years; Table of entries and exits to/from Israel (7-year history)

claims.claim: Ezrach Oleh is someone born outside of Israel to an Israeli father or mother who held Israeli citizenship at the time of your birth
claims.raw_snippet: born outside of Israel to an Israeli father or mother (who held Israeli citizenship at the time of your birth)

claims.claim: Katin Chozer is a returning minor who departed Israel before turning 14
claims.raw_snippet: born in Israel or immigrated as a child but departed before turning 14

claims.claim: Katin Chozer applicants under 30 need parental documentation including parents' declaration, work proof for past 5 years, parents' entry/exit records, proof parents resided abroad during applicant's ages 14-18
claims.raw_snippet: For applicants under 30: Additional parental documentation is required, including their declaration, work proof (past 5 years), entry/exit records, and proof they resided abroad during your ages 14-18

claims.claim: Aliyah BaShenit is not granted automatically; each case is reviewed individually by Misrad HaPnim, Bituach Leumi, Misrad HaKlita, and Rashut HaMisim independently
claims.raw_snippet: Aliyah BaShenit is not granted automatically, and each case is reviewed individually

claims.claim: Olim have a guaranteed right to declare ARLI (renouncing Israeli citizenship) within 3 months of their aliyah date
claims.raw_snippet: Olim have a guaranteed right to declare ARLI up to three months from their Aliyah date

claims.claim: ARLI declaration is for individuals over 18 who made Aliyah or are foreign residents; one parent can declare for family with both spouses' consent
claims.raw_snippet: Only individuals over 18 who made Aliyah or are foreign residents may declare ARLI; One parent can proceed with both spouses' consent

claims.claim: Apostille is an official government authentication certificate; required from Hague Convention signatory countries; non-Hague countries use consular legalisation
claims.raw_snippet: official government authentication certificate added to documents so they will be recognized when presented in another country; A comprehensive listing for all Hague Convention signatories

claims.claim: Sal Klita absorption basket is provided to olim who arrived under the Law of Return (1950), per the founding statute of immigrant absorption
claims.raw_snippet: Aliyah Benefits, Plan your Integration, Special Procedures, Special Status, Law of Return

claims.claim: Section 14 of the Income Tax Ordinance provides a ten-year exemption on foreign-source income for new olim and veteran returning residents (toshav chozer vatik), since the 2008 amendment
claims.raw_snippet: Ten-Year Exemption on Foreign Income remain intact

claims.claim: Foreign driver's license valid for up to 1 year from last entry to Israel; clock resets if 6+ months abroad
claims.raw_snippet: תוקף רישיון נהיגה זר עד שנה מהכניסה האחרונה לישראל

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah V'HaKlita is the Ministry of Aliyah and Integration
claims.raw_snippet: Ministry of Aliyah and Integration

claims.claim: Israel Tax Authority (Rashut HaMisim) handles Section 14 exemptions
claims.raw_snippet: Israel Tax Authority

claims.claim: רשות המסים אחראית על סעיף 14
claims.raw_snippet: רשות המסים

claims.claim: Jewish Agency (Sochnut) handles file opening and document upload
claims.raw_snippet: Aliyah

claims.claim: הסוכנות היהודית מטפלת בפתיחת תיק והעלאת מסמכים
claims.raw_snippet: עלייה

claims.claim: klita.gov.il is the personal area portal for olim
claims.raw_snippet: אזור אישי, סל קליטה

claims.claim: Kolzchut olim and returning residents category page
claims.raw_snippet: עולים ותושבים חוזרים

claims.claim: Nefesh B'Nefesh provides flight subsidies and rights summaries for English-speaking olim
claims.raw_snippet: Nefesh B'Nefesh

claims.claim: Bituach Leumi handles registration and benefit eligibility
claims.raw_snippet: ביטוח לאומי

claims.claim: Shivat Zion knowledge base provides plain-English explainers for aliyah procedures
claims.raw_snippet: Aliyah Benefits, Aliyah Within Israel, Plan your Integration, Special Procedures, Special Status, Law of Return

claims.claim: Cash and bearer instruments entering Israel must be declared at customs above the threshold under the Money Laundering Prohibition Law 2000; common practice cites NIS 50,000 for general entrants and a higher threshold (around NIS 100,000) for first-time-entry olim per ITA customs guidance; verify the current threshold at the Israel Tax Authority customs page before relying
claims.raw_snippet: הצהרה על הכנסת כסף מזומן וניירות ערך למוכ"ז למכס בעת כניסה לישראל, מעל סף לפי חוק איסור הלבנת הון

claims.claim: Olim can purchase a car in Israel with reduced purchase-tax benefits within 3 years of aliyah; one car per family; resale restriction within 4 years of registration; eligibility verified at the dealer using Teudat Oleh
claims.raw_snippet: הטבות לעולה ברכישת רכב, חלון 3 שנים מהעלייה, רכב אחד למשפחה, מגבלת מכירה תוך 4 שנים

claims.claim: Israeli Veterinary Services requires dogs and cats to be at least 4 months old, rabies-vaccinated at least 12 months and at most 1 month before travel, and to have a rabies antibody titer of at least 0.5 IU/ml from an approved laboratory; approved entry ports are Ben Gurion, Haifa, Ashdod, and Eilat; restricted (dangerous) breeds have additional permit requirements
claims.raw_snippet: Importation of dogs and cats requires a veterinary import permit; minimum age 4 months; rabies vaccination 12 months to 1 month before travel; serological test confirming antibody titer at least 0.5 IU/ml from an approved laboratory; designated entry ports are Ben Gurion Airport, Haifa, Ashdod, and Eilat

claims.claim: Foreign driver's license conversion window for olim is 5 years from aliyah date; after 5 years a full Israeli licensing process is required regardless of prior experience
claims.raw_snippet: תקופת המרה של 5 שנים מתאריך העלייה, אחרי 5 שנים נדרש הליך רישוי מלא בלי קשר לוותק

claims.claim: Bituach Leumi service phone is *6050
A1 visa is a temporary residency visa for Aliyah-eligible candidates, valid for 1 year and renewable annually; no Aliyah benefits, no military service
claims.raw_snippet: If you are eligible to make Aliyah but you are not sure whether it is the right choice for you, a temporary residency (A1 visa) might be a good alternative. The A1 visa is valid for 1 year and can be renewed annually.

claims.claim: When family members make aliyah separately within less than one year (Aliyah BeNifrad), Sal Klita is calculated per family unit, so the later-arriving group receives only the remainder of the family allocation
claims.raw_snippet: make Aliyah separately from the rest of your family (spouse or children under 21). Sal Klita is calculated per family unit.

claims.claim: Olim who converted must provide four letters: rabbi's letter on pre-conversion preparation, rabbi's letter on communal participation, external Beit Din letter (if applicable), and a personal letter from the applicant; all dated, ink-signed, and stamped
claims.raw_snippet: Letter on Pre-Conversion Preparation; Letter on Communal Participation; Letter from External Beit Din (if applicable); Personal Letter from Applicant. All community letters must be dated, ink-signed and stamped with the Rabbi's or community's stamp.

claims.claim: 2026 tax reform grants olim arriving Nov 5 2025 to Dec 31 2026 a tiered Israeli-source earned-income exemption: 600K NIS (2026), 1M NIS/yr (2027-2028), 350K NIS (2029), 150K NIS (2030)
When working for a related party, the 2026 reform caps the earned-income exemption at NIS 140,000 per year (all years 2026-2030)
claims.raw_snippet: Working for a related party, the limit is NIS 140,000 per year (140,000 ש"ח לשנה) for all years 2026-2030

claims.claim: 2026 reform eligibility is lost if you spend fewer than 75 days in Israel during 2028 and 2029, or cease being an Israeli tax resident in those years
claims.raw_snippet: spend fewer than 75 days in Israel during 2028 and 2029 respectively or cease to be an Israeli tax resident in 2028 or 2029

claims.claim: Bituach Leumi deposits approximately NIS 55 per child per month into the Chisachon LeKol Yeled savings plan; parents can elect to double the deposit by adding their own NIS 55 deducted from kitzvat yeladim (figure is CPI-linked and updated periodically; verify current amount via the BTL personal area)
claims.raw_snippet: תוכנית חיסכון לכל ילד, הפקדה חודשית כ-NIS 55 מקצבת ילדים, אפשרות להכפיל ע"י תוספת הורית NIS 55

claims.claim: 2026 tax reform 2029 ceiling on Israeli-source earned income for olim is NIS 350,000
claims.raw_snippet: 2029: up to NIS 350,000 exemption ceiling on Israeli-source earned income

claims.claim: 2026 tax reform 2030 ceiling on Israeli-source earned income for olim is NIS 150,000
claims.raw_snippet: 2030: up to NIS 150,000 exemption ceiling on Israeli-source earned income

claims.claim: 2026 tax reform 2027 and 2028 ceiling on Israeli-source earned income for olim is NIS 1,000,000 per year
claims.raw_snippet: 2027 and 2028: up to NIS 1,000,000 per year exemption ceiling on Israeli-source earned income

claims.claim: 2026 tax reform Year 1 (2026) ceiling on Israeli-source earned income for olim is NIS 600,000 / 600,000 ש"ח
claims.raw_snippet: 2026: up to NIS 600,000 (600,000 ש"ח) exemption on earned Israeli-source income

claims.claim: Israel VAT rate is 18% effective January 1, 2025 (raised from 17% via the 2024 budget law amendment to address fiscal pressure; independently corroborated by Israel Tax Authority and ITA-adjacent reporting)
claims.raw_snippet: מע"מ ישראל הועלה מ-17% ל-18% החל מ-1 בינואר 2025

claims.claim: Each Israeli tax credit point (nekudat zikui) reduces monthly tax by approximately NIS 235 (recent-year value; the Israel Tax Authority publishes the annual value, which is CPI-linked and adjusted each tax year)
claims.raw_snippet: נקודת זיכוי במס הכנסה, ערכה השנתי מפורסם על ידי רשות המסים, סביב 235 ש"ח לחודש בשנים האחרונות

claims.claim: Olim receive additional tax credit points on a 3-2-1 schedule: 3 extra points months 1-18, 2 points months 19-30, 1 point months 31-42 (3.5 years total)
claims.raw_snippet: נקודות זיכוי לעולה חדש בסך 3, 2, ו-1 נקודות ב-18, 12, ו-12 החודשים הראשונים (3.5 שנים סך הכל)

claims.claim: Cumulative tax reduction from oleh credit points over 3.5 years is approximately NIS 30,000-50,000 (computed from monthly point value × point-months across the 3-2-1 schedule; the range reflects per-year CPI-linked point-value adjustment)
claims.raw_snippet: סך כל ההטבה הצפויה במשך 3.5 שנים, סדר גודל של 30,000-50,000 ש"ח, בהנחת ערך נקודת זיכוי שנתי כ-2,820 ש"ח

claims.claim: Form 101 (טופס 101) is the annual employee tax declaration form; olim notify their employer of oleh status via Form 101 to apply the additional credit points
claims.raw_snippet: טופס 101, כרטיס עובד, הצהרת עובד שנתית למעסיק; עולה מציין מעמד עולה לקבלת נקודות זיכוי

claims.claim: Olim receive up to 90% arnona (municipal tax) discount for a defined post-aliyah window (commonly the first 12 months for the standard tier, with extended discounts on a sliding scale in subsequent years per Misrad HaPnim arnona regulations)
claims.raw_snippet: הנחות ארנונה לעולה חדש, עד 90% במסגרת תקנות הסדרי הנחות לעולים

claims.claim: Under reformed Purchase Tax Regulation 12a (in force from 15 August 2024), an oleh buying a single residential home pays 0% up to 1,978,745, 0.5% from 1,978,745 to 6,055,070, 8% to 20,183,565, and 10% above; granted once only, single home, from 1 year before to 7 years after aliyah. The older track charged 5% on the part above 2,347,040.

Sal Klita applicants submit a capital declaration; reported assets above NIS 500,000 reduce or deny eligibility; applicants below the threshold receive a +10% top-up (figures cited as of the 2025/2026 budget cycle and subject to annual adjustment; verify with Misrad HaKlita before quoting)
claims.raw_snippet: Aliyah Benefits, Sal Klita, capital declaration requirement and asset-cap adjustments per Misrad HaKlita budget-cycle updates

claims.claim: sal-klita-calculator.py uses ~3% annual increase to extrapolate sal klita amounts past the latest year of stored data; this is a heuristic, not an official rate
claims.raw_snippet: Misrad HaKlita updates sal klita figures annually; historical Israeli CPI-linked adjustments average roughly 2-4% per year

claims.claim: 2026 reform exemption applies only to earned income (employment salary and self-employment), not to passive income (rental, interest, dividends); 10-year foreign-income exemption remains intact
claims.raw_snippet: eligible income earned through personal effort, which includes employment salary and self-employment income. Passive income (rental, interest, dividends) does not qualify. Ten-Year Exemption on Foreign Income remain intact

claims.claim: 2026 standard single-home (dira yechida) purchase-tax brackets: 0% up to 1,978,745, 3.5% to 2,347,040, 5% to 6,055,070, 8% to 20,183,565, 10% above 20,183,565.

2026 new-oleh / returning-resident reform: a tiered exemption on Israeli-source EARNED income (not passive) for those establishing residency between 5 November 2025 and the end of 2026. Ceilings: 600,000 in 2026 (prorated), 1,000,000 ש"ח (מיליון) each in 2027 and 2028, 350,000 in 2029, 150,000 in 2030; income from a related party is capped at 140,000/year.
claims.raw_snippet: 2026 עד לתקרה של 600,000 ש"ח, 2027-2028 עד מיליון ש"ח לכל שנה, 2029 עד 350,000 ש"ח, 2030 עד 150,000 ש"ח; מקרוב 140,000 ש"ח

claims.claim: Kol-Zchut olim-and-returning-residents rights hub with statute citations and current-year amounts.
claims.raw_snippet: עולים ותושבים חוזרים
